message:
Automatically switch .docs to main after topic branch merges

When a .docs topic branch is merged, the synchronization hook now
automatically switches back to main and pulls the latest changes.
Previously, only a warning was issued, leaving the repository on a
stale merged branch that caused docs:write to work with outdated
documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+---
+title: Automatic branch switching after merged documentation branches
+type: bugfix
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2026-01-17T10:23:52.103415Z
+---
+
+The `.docs` directory now automatically switches to `main` when a topic branch has been merged, preventing stale documentation from being used during editing. Previously, the synchronization hook would only warn about merged branches but leave the repository on the stale branch, causing `docs:write` to work with outdated content.

@@ -120,7 +120,14 @@ if [[ "$current_branch" == "main" ]]; then
 else
   # On a topic branch
   if is_branch_merged "$current_branch"; then
-    warn "Branch '$current_branch' was merged to main, consider switching to main"
+    warn "Branch '$current_branch' was merged to main, switching to main"
+    git -C "$DOCS_DIR" switch main 2>/dev/null || {
+      warn "Failed to switch to main"
+      exit 0
+    }
+    git -C "$DOCS_DIR" pull --ff-only origin main 2>/dev/null || {
+      warn "Failed to pull latest changes"
+    }
   elif ! would_merge_cleanly; then
     block "origin/main has changes that conflict with branch '$current_branch'. Rebase onto origin/main to resolve."
   fi
